template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS HEALTHCARE PROXY**:
Statutory health-care decision-making instrument under M.G.L. c. 201D, which Massachusetts adopted in 1990. It appoints a Health Care Agent empowered to make ALL health care decisions (except those the Principal reserves) in the event the attending physician determines in writing that the Principal lacks capacity to make or communicate health care decisions. Unlike the DPOA, the Healthcare Proxy is governed by a separate chapter and has distinct execution requirements. Massachusetts does NOT have a separate statutory 'living will' form — advance-directive instructions are combined with the Proxy or provided in a separate letter of wishes. Target length: 2–4 pages. Two witnesses required; notarization NOT required.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `successorAgents`: integer — number of successor agents (typically 1–2).
- `includeLivingWillInstructions`: boolean (default true) — include written instructions on life-sustaining treatment, artificial nutrition/hydration, pain management, organ donation.
- `reserveDecisions`: array — any decisions the Principal reserves (e.g., `mental_health_commitment`, `sterilization`, `abortion` — note MA's constitutional limitations).
- `polstLink`: boolean — when true, reference the MOLST (Medical Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment) form for persons with serious advanced illness.

**STRUCTURE**:

## MASSACHUSETTS HEALTH CARE PROXY

### I. IDENTIFICATION
I, [Principal full legal name], of [address], residing in [city/town], [County] County, Massachusetts, being an adult of sound mind, hereby execute this Health Care Proxy pursuant to M.G.L. c. 201D.

### II. DESIGNATION OF HEALTH CARE AGENT
I appoint [Primary Agent full legal name], of [address], phone [number], as my Health Care Agent to make any and all health care decisions on my behalf, except to the extent I state otherwise in this document, if I am unable to make them myself.

### III. SUCCESSOR AGENT(S)
If [Primary Agent] is unable, unwilling, or unavailable to serve, I appoint:
- **First Successor**: [Successor 1 full legal name], of [address], phone [number].
- **Second Successor**: [Successor 2 full legal name], of [address], phone [number].

### IV. EFFECTIVENESS
This Health Care Proxy becomes effective if and when my attending physician determines in writing, and documents in my medical record, that I lack the capacity to make or communicate health care decisions. If I later regain capacity, my Agent's authority automatically suspends until capacity is again lost, consistent with M.G.L. c. 201D, § 6.

### V. SCOPE OF AGENT'S AUTHORITY
My Agent shall have the authority to make any and all health care decisions that I could make for myself if I had capacity, including but not limited to:
(a) Consenting to, refusing, or withdrawing any type of health care, treatment, service, or procedure;
(b) Accessing, reviewing, and receiving copies of my medical records, including protected health information under HIPAA (45 C.F.R. § 164.508; § 164.502(g));
(c) Employing and dismissing health care providers;
(d) Authorizing admission to or transfer between hospitals, nursing homes, assisted living facilities, hospice programs, and similar institutions;
(e) Making decisions regarding pain management;
(f) Making decisions regarding mental health treatment, to the extent permitted under Massachusetts law [if not reserved];
(g) Consenting to autopsy, anatomical gifts, and the disposition of remains, consistent with M.G.L. c. 113, § 8 (Uniform Anatomical Gift Act) and M.G.L. c. 114.

My Agent shall make decisions in accordance with my expressed wishes, if known, or, if my wishes are not known, in accordance with my best interests as determined by my Agent in good faith.

### VI. [IF reserveDecisions] RESERVED DECISIONS
Notwithstanding the foregoing, I reserve the following decisions to myself and do NOT delegate them to my Agent: [list reserved decisions — e.g., mental health commitment decisions under M.G.L. c. 123, § 35 or § 12].

### VII. [IF includeLivingWillInstructions] INSTRUCTIONS AND STATEMENT OF WISHES
Although Massachusetts does not provide a statutory living will, I direct my Agent and any health care provider to consider the following instructions as evidence of my wishes:

**§ 7.1 — Life-Sustaining Treatment**
[Select from standard options or customize]:
- **Option A — Full Code**: I want all medically appropriate life-sustaining treatment to be provided, including cardiopulmonary resuscitation, mechanical ventilation, dialysis, and other life-support measures, for as long as they offer a reasonable probability of recovery or meaningful benefit.
- **Option B — Limited / Terminal Condition**: If I have a terminal condition (defined as an incurable and irreversible illness that, without the administration of life-sustaining treatment, will, in the opinion of my attending physician, result in death within a relatively short time), I do NOT want life-sustaining treatment that would merely prolong the dying process. I want to receive care that provides comfort and dignity.
- **Option C — Permanent Unconsciousness**: If I am in a permanent vegetative state or permanent unconsciousness from which there is no reasonable medical probability of recovery, I do NOT want life-sustaining treatment, including artificial nutrition and hydration, to be continued.

**§ 7.2 — Artificial Nutrition and Hydration**
[Select]: I [do / do not] wish to receive artificial nutrition and hydration when I cannot swallow or take nutrition orally, under the conditions described in § 7.1.

**§ 7.3 — Comfort Care and Pain Management**
I expressly direct that I receive aggressive pain management and comfort care at all times, including the administration of narcotics in whatever quantity is necessary to alleviate suffering, even if such medication may have the secondary effect of shortening my life.

**§ 7.4 — Organ and Tissue Donation**
[Select]: I [wish / do not wish] to donate my organs and tissues for transplantation, therapy, research, or education, consistent with the Uniform Anatomical Gift Act, M.G.L. c. 113A.

### VIII. [IF polstLink] MEDICAL ORDERS FOR LIFE-SUSTAINING TREATMENT (MOLST)
I have also executed / intend to execute a Massachusetts MOLST form, which shall be honored as a medical order by emergency medical services and health care facilities. The MOLST form is a companion document to this Health Care Proxy and does not supersede it.

### IX. HIPAA AUTHORIZATION
I hereby authorize my Health Care Agent to receive, review, and disclose any and all of my protected health information, including records maintained under HIPAA (45 C.F.R. Parts 160 and 164), from any health care provider, consistent with 45 C.F.R. § 164.502(g)(2) (treatment of a personal representative as the individual for purposes of HIPAA).

### X. REVOCATION
I may revoke this Health Care Proxy at any time by: (a) notifying my Agent, a health care provider, or any witness orally or in writing of my intent to revoke; (b) executing a subsequent Health Care Proxy; or (c) by any act evidencing a specific intent to revoke, consistent with M.G.L. c. 201D, § 7. Revocation is effective upon communication to the Agent or to any health care provider.

### XI. GOVERNING LAW
This Health Care Proxy shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, specifically M.G.L. c. 201D.

### EXECUTION

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have signed this Health Care Proxy on this _____ day of ________________, 20___.

_______________________________
[Principal full legal name]

### WITNESSES (TWO ADULTS REQUIRED)
The undersigned witnesses declare that the Principal appears to be at least eighteen (18) years of age, of sound mind, and under no constraint or undue influence. Neither witness is the person named as Health Care Agent or Successor Agent in this instrument.

Witness 1:
_______________________________
Printed name, address, and date

Witness 2:
_______________________________
Printed name, address, and date

### [OPTIONAL] AGENT ACCEPTANCE
I, [Primary Agent name], accept appointment as Health Care Agent for [Principal] under this Health Care Proxy. I understand the scope and gravity of this responsibility and agree to make health care decisions in accordance with the Principal's wishes, if known, or otherwise in the Principal's best interests.

_______________________________
[Agent signature and date]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 201D, § 1 et seq.`. Key sections: § 2 (execution), § 6 (effectiveness / capacity determination), § 7 (revocation).
- TWO ADULT WITNESSES are required; witnesses must NOT be the named Agent or Successor. Notarization is NOT required.
- Massachusetts does NOT have a statutory living-will form — instructions are included here under § VII or in a separate letter of wishes referenced by the Proxy.
- Health Care Agent under c. 201D is a DIFFERENT instrument from Durable Power of Attorney under c. 190B. The DPOA does NOT confer health-care decision-making authority and the Proxy does NOT confer financial authority — both are needed for comprehensive incapacity planning.
- MOLST (Medical Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment) is a separate companion form recognized under 105 C.M.R. § 125.000 — it functions as a medical order, not an advance directive; reference but do not replicate inside the Proxy.
- The HIPAA authorization language must reference `45 C.F.R. § 164.502(g)(2)` to be effective under federal privacy rules.
- Target length: 2–4 pages.
hints: Massachusetts Health Care Proxy under M.G.L. c. 201D. Effective ONLY when attending physician documents incapacity (§ 6). Two witnesses required (NOT the Agent); notarization NOT required. MA has no statutory living will — advance directive instructions are included in the Proxy or in a separate letter of wishes. MOLST (105 CMR 125.000) is a separate medical order form for seriously ill persons. HIPAA authorization under 45 C.F.R. § 164.502(g)(2). Anatomical gifts under M.G.L. c. 113A (UAGA). Revocation by oral statement to Agent / provider / witness per § 7. DPOA (c. 190B) is a separate instrument for financial matters — both are needed.
